The Being mode is the mode by which you are undergoing a transformative interaction with something in contrast with the [[having mode]].

Trying to satisfy the being mode with the [[having mode]] is the source of [[forgetting]]. There is a second more obscure form of modal confusion where one does the other way but it is unclear whether it is a particular problem

== Quote ==
The being mode is the [[co-identification]] that happens when you’re trying to meet your developmental needs. These needs are not met by controlling and consuming things. They are met by becoming by coming into being.<ref name=":0" />

For example: I need to become mature, I need to be in love, I need to be courageous, I need to become kinder. These needs are not met by having things but by going through transformations.<ref name=":0" />

The concept was introduced by [[Erich Fromm]].
[[Category:John Vervaeke]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Being mode]][[Category:Forgetting]]
<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|title=Being Mode - Awakening from the Meaning Crisis|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210307140334/https://awakeningfromthemeaningcrisis.com/being-mode|website=[[awakeningfromthemeaningcrisis.com]]|date=2021-03-07|access-date=2022-11-10}}</ref>

==References==

<references />[[Category:Oldest pages before the refresh]]
[[Category:Oldest pages before the refresh]]
[[Category:Pages]]
